math_recheck: Re-derived against Williams JACM 2014 (refs/acc-lbs-jacm.pdf): modulus-amplifying polynomial P_k and Q_k=1-P_k(x^{p-1}) directions, CRT split + MOD_{p^e} via p|C(x,p^i), Valiant-Vazirani/MAJORITY/Transformation order, zeta-transform DP recurrence (g_i(T)=g_{i-1}(T)+g_{i-1}(T\{i}) if i in T), Coppersmith N x N^.1 dims, efficient Cook-Levin n+O(log n) inputs, Lemma 4.1 size s^{O(log^{f(d,m)}s)} with f(d,m)<=m^{O(d)}, Evaluation Lemma (2^n+poly(s))poly(n), and the k-blowup exponent arithmetic K<=2^{n^{2/3}} with the running time 2^{n-Omega(n^{1/(2e)})}; E^NP binary-search witness S(3n), metatheorem Thm 1.3, and sub-third-exponential/grotesque-class extensions all match the source.
fixes: reasoning.md:373 SYM+ size exponent K <= s^{e(k^e * log_e s)} was wrong (substituted blowup param k^e for the second constant e, and base-e log instead of e-th power of log); corrected to s^{e(e * log^e s)} matching the paper's s^{e(e log^e s)}

reasoning.md:374 K <= 2^{e n^ε (k^e n^{εe})} corrected to 2^{e n^ε (e n^{εe})} (paper's 2^{e n^ε(e n^{εe})}), and added a correct note that the 2^k blowup factor only raises log s' from n^ε to n^ε+n^{1/(2e)}, keeping the exponent below n^{2/3}
